lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Asking for Help & Solving Problems
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tânia Rosa | Aula 19 -- Asking for Help & Solving Problems

listenings.text: Good afternoon. I am calling because I have a problem with my room. The air conditioning is broken, and the hot water is missing too. I tried to call reception twice, but nobody answered. I would like to ask the staff to fix it today, or to replace my room with another one. I am sorry to complain, but I really cannot stay like this. Could you please sort this out as soon as possible? Thank you for your help.

Hello, this is the airline service desk. I understand your flight was delayed by four hours, and I want to apologize for the mistake. Here is my advice: you should keep your boarding pass and your receipts, because you had better ask for a refund or a free meal. You should not throw anything away. If your luggage is still missing tomorrow, you should call this number again. We will do our best to sort everything out for you.

extra_audio.text: First, I arrived at the hotel and the staff gave me a broken key. Then, I noticed the air conditioning was missing, so I made a complaint. After that, the manager said sorry and offered to replace my room. As soon as I moved, everything was fine. Luckily, they also gave me a small refund for the mistake. Eventually, my trip became great again.
